change_your_default_payment_methods_001:
# Change Your Default Payment Method

# Change Your Default Payment Method

You can select your default payment method in Your Payments.

Select or change your default payment method in

Your Wallet

**How to change your default payment method**

- Go to Your Wallet. If you already have a default payment method selected, it will be listed first.
- Select the payment method to set as the default. Select Edit.
- Select the box Set as default payment method. You may need to complete authentication to default
  your payment method.
- Select Save.
- If you have existing subscriptions, you may be prompted to update your account. Select
  **Update**.

**Not what you're searching for? Try:**

- Change Your Order Information
- Update Your Payment Method
- Resolve a Declined Payment
- Check the Status of Your Refund
- Unknown Amazon Payment Charges

check_your_gift_card_balance_001:
# Check Your Gift Card Balance

# Check Your Gift Card Balance

You can check your Amazon.com Gift Card balance in Your Account.

When you apply an Amazon.com Gift Card to an order or add one to Your Account for future purchases, we store the available balance so you can check it at any time.

To find your Amazon.com Gift Card balance in Your Account:

1. Go to Your Account.
2. Select Gift cards and check the balance on any of your Amazon.com Gift Cards.

**Note:**

- Some physical gift cards bought from Amazon.com don't come with values printed on them. In this case, you can check the value by
  going to Your Orders. In Your Orders, find the gift card order, and compare the 16-digit serial number (located beside
  the gift card amount) in the order details to the serial number on the back of the
  gift card.
- You can check all changes to your balance in the transaction section of your Balance Page, including order IDs or serial numbers.
- Your Gift Card balance doesn’t include possible promotional certificates added to
  your account.
- Please check the Amazon Gift Card Terms and Conditions to make sure that your item is eligible, when your balance is not provided as payment
  method during checkout.

check_your_credit_and_benefit_balances_0:
# Check Your Credit and Benefit Balances

# Check Your Credit and Benefit Balances

You can find information on your promotions, credit, and benefit balances in
Your Account.

Enter the prerequisites here (optional).

To check your promotional credits and balance:

1. Go to
   Your Account.
2. Select **Your credit & benefit balances**.

You can also go directly to
Your credit & benefit balances.

**Note:** Data for credit activity is only available for 365 days.

manage_payment_methods_001:
# Manage Payment Methods

# Manage Payment Methods

Add or update your payment methods through Your Payments in Your Account.

To manage your payment methods:

1. In Your Account, select Your Payments.
2. Choose one of the following:
   - To add a payment method, select Add a payment method.
   - To edit a payment method, select the card you wish to edit. When the card opens,
     select Edit. You can update your billing address or update card details.
   - To remove a payment method, select the card you wish to remove. When the card opens,
     select Remove from wallet.
3. Follow the on-screen instructions.

You can also add a new credit/debit card to your account while placing an order through
the shopping cart; when on the Select a Payment Method page, choose Add a credit or debit card and enter your details. The new payment method will be saved to your account and
appear as a saved credit/debit card when you place your next Amazon order.

Updating a payment method in Your Payments will not change the payment method on open orders. To change the payment method for
an open order, go to Change Your Order Information.

**Note:** Certain Health Savings Accounts (HSA) cards cannot be registered as Flexible Spending
Accounts (FSA) or HSA cards with Amazon but can still be used if they're registered
as credit cards. If you register your HSA card as a credit card, you'll be responsible
for ensuring the card is used only for eligible products. HSA cards that are registered
as credit cards do not automatically restrict the types of products you can purchase
with the card. Purchasing ineligible products or services with the card may require
you to report them and pay additional taxes. Contact your plan administrator or employer
for more information.

- With Tap to Pay, you can add a contactless card to your wallet to pay for your order.
  Use the Amazon app with a compatible device for Tap to Pay transactions. There are
  no transaction limits. Select payment method at checkout for the Tap to Pay feature to appear.
- Instructions will advise you to tap your contactless payment card to your phone to
  complete the order. Once successful, you'll be notified.

**Note:** In some instances, card issuers may provide Amazon with updated payment card details
when reissuing cards. For metal cards, the NFC antenna for tapping may be located
towards the rear of the card.

manage_thirdparty_apps_and_services_with:
# Manage third-party apps & services with data access through Login with Amazon

# Manage third-party apps & services with data access through Login with Amazon

Login with Amazon allows you to easily and securely use your Amazon account to sign
in to and share selected data based on your consent with third-party websites or apps.
If you no longer want to allow third-party apps or services to access your data, you
can remove their connection to your Amazon account.

To remove third party access:

1. Make sure you're signed into the Amazon Account.
2. Go to Your Account > Manage your data > Manage apps & services with data access.
3. Select Remove next to the appor service whose connection you want to remove.

   **Note:** Even if you remove access, the service can still use the information you previously provided. If you no longer want to get
   emails from a website or app using Login with Amazon, contact the provider directly
   for their unsubscription procedure.

manage_your_amazon_accounts_on_mobile_de:
# Manage Your Amazon Accounts on Mobile Devices

# Manage Your Amazon Accounts on Mobile Devices

You can add, remove, or switch between Amazon accounts using the Amazon shopping
app on your mobile device.

**Note:** Do not add your Amazon accounts to a device that is public, or shared with unknown
people, because anyone with access to the device will also have access to your Amazon
accounts. Any new Amazon accounts added to your mobile app inherit the app permissions,
such as camera roll access, or preferences, such as language, automatically. Updating
app settings on one Amazon account updates them for all.

To manage your Amazon account on a mobile device:

1. In your Amazon shopping app, go to Menu.
2. Go to Settings and select Switch Accounts.

When signing out on your device, you'll be signed out only on this device.

If you need to sign out from a device you no longer have access to, sign into your
Amazon account on a desktop or mobile browser, and visit the Manage Your Content and Devices page. Under the Devices tab, select your device in the list, and select Deregister.

manage_your_backup_payment_methods_001:
# Manage Your Backup Payment Methods

# Manage Your Backup Payment Methods

Backup payment methods help us avoid payment delays. You can manage your backup payment
methods through Your Payments in Your Account.

To add or update a backup payment method, go to

Manage backup payment method

If there's a problem with your payment, we'll automatically charge a backup card from
Your Payments for eligible products and services to make sure we fulfill our delivery promises

To manage your backup payment methods:

1. Choose enabled or disabled. Note that disabling backup payment methods may cause delays
   if your payment method fails.
2. Review and update your payment methods. Uncheck any you don't want to use as backup
   payment methods.
3. Select Save.

To change your payment method after your back up payment method is charged for that
order:

1. Go to Customer Service.
2. Select the order where the backup payment method was used.
3. Click on Change payment method.
4. Select a different preferred payment method.
5. Click on Confirm modification.

**Note:** The refund may take 7-10 business days to return to your payment method.

**Not what you're searching for? Try:**

- Manage Payment Methods
- Resolve a Declined Payment
- Change Your Order Information

request_the_closure_of_your_account_and_:
# Request the Closure of Your Account and the Deletion of Your Personal Information

# Request the Closure of Your Account and the Deletion of Your Personal Information

You can submit a request for us to close your Amazon account and delete your personal
information permanently.


Follow these steps to submit a request to close your Amazon account and delete your
personal information:

1. Go to
   Close Your Amazon Account.
2. Sign into the account you want to close.
3. Review the products and services associated with your account.
4. If you still wish to proceed, you can select a reason in the drop-down menu. Then
   select the box next to
   Yes, I want to permanently close my Amazon account and delete my data. To complete the closure, select
   Close my Account.
5. A confirmation notification will be sent to the email address associated with your
   account or via text message. You’ll need to reply within five days to verify your
   request.

## Consequences of Closing Your Account

- Once your account has been closed, all products and services accessed through your
  account will no longer be available to you, across any Amazon site globally. For example,
  submitting your account closure request through this website will also close your
  account on
  amazon.com,
  amazon.fr,
  amazon.com.mx, and all other global sites that use the same credentials to access services and
  products offered through those sites.
- Once your Amazon account is closed, you will not be able to access and manage any
  of your business accounts – such as Seller accounts, Brand Registry accounts, or Advertising
  accounts – as well as other products and services included in the closed accounts.
- Once your account is closed, it is no longer accessible to you and it cannot be restored.
  If you decide later that you want to start ordering from us again, or if you would
  like to use website features that require an account, you'll need to create a new
  account. To learn more, visit
  What Happens When I Close My Account?

## Multiple Accounts

- If you have multiple accounts, you need to follow the listed steps for each of the
  accounts. This ensures that we have the correct authority to take action on each account
  you wish to close.

## Additional Support

- If you are experiencing issues with logging into the account you wish to close, go
  to
  Why Can't I Log into My Account? for further assistance.
- If you do not have an account, or if you are an authorized agent under applicable
  state law,
  email us

sign_out_of_your_amazon_account_in_the_a:
# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

You can sign out of your Amazon account using the following steps.

To sign out of your Amazon account in the Amazon shopping app on Android and iOS mobile
devices:

1. Open the app and select the hamburger button on the bottom right corner of the screen.
2. Scroll down to the bottom of the page.
3. Select Sign-out and confirm.

sign_out_of_your_amazon_account_on_the_a:
# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

# Sign out of Your Amazon Account in the Amazon Shopping App

To sign out of your Amazon account in the Amazon shopping app on Android and iOS mobile

2. Scroll down to the bottom of the page.
3. Select Sign-out and confirm.
